mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending demand contraction, causing investors to price in lower earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during confirmed recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue visibility and supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or equity returns are flat when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate stays above 4%, real rates are constrained and policy uncertainty peaks, compressing equity risk premiums and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both CPI and funds rate exceed thresholds, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP provides a direct measure of aggregate economic activity, with positive GDP surprises or sustained expansion historically translating into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no correlation with GDP growth direction over a 5-year sample, or negative GDP quarters do not systematically precede equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index exceeding 105 reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which supports margin expansion and drives positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in > 50% of instances when INDPRO crosses above 105, or the 5-day momentum effect vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls coupled with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market without overheating, reducing recession fears and supporting positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when payrolls rise and unemployment stabilizes, or the interaction effect is dominated by a single series in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining residential investment alongside falling housing starts indicates a credit-driven demand shock, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly period.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when both residential investment and housing starts decline simultaneously, or the signal's predictive power disappears after adjusting for interest rate changes.
